# third_party

Vendored material from upstream projects, used as reference and as raw
test-fixture input. All three upstream projects are MPL-2.0, and each
subdirectory keeps its upstream `LICENSE`.

This project as a whole is a port of erengy/anitomy and is distributed under
the MPL-2.0 (see the repository root `LICENSE`), so the vendored fixtures and
the ported code share the same license, and there is no cross-license
boundary here.

## anitomy-develop/ (github.com/erengy/anitomy, `develop` branch — upstream's default)

The live C++23 rewrite. This is the branch upstream's README and default
checkout point to.

- `test_data.json` — upstream's own conformance fixture (250 cases), copied
  verbatim from `test/data.json`. Keys already match the current
  `ElementKind` names (snake_case).
- `element.hpp.ref`, `options.hpp.ref`, `format.hpp.ref` — copies of the
  current public API headers (`ElementKind`, `Element`, `Options`, and the
  kind <-> string name tables), kept here so the interface can be checked
  against upstream without re-cloning. Not built or executed.

## anitomy-master/ (github.com/erengy/anitomy, `master` branch — the original, frozen implementation)

The pre-rewrite C++14-era implementation (`anitomy.cpp`/`parser.cpp`/etc.,
`wchar_t`-based, old `ElementCategory` enum). Not the branch upstream's
tooling defaults to, but a separate, older conformance target: it predates
the rewrite-only features (e.g. `Part` doesn't exist in this branch at all)
and heuristics, so its bundled fixture checks against a different baseline
than `develop`'s.

- `test_data.json` — copied verbatim from this branch's own `test/data.json`
  (182 cases). Old schema: `anime_title`, `episode_number`,
  `anime_season_prefix`, etc. — same vocabulary anitopy uses (see below),
  since anitopy was ported from roughly this era of anitomy.

## anitopy/ (github.com/igorcmoura/anitopy)

Python port of an older anitomy version. Its own test data is itself "took
from anitomy" per its source comments, using the same *old* element-category
names as `anitomy-master/`.

- `table.py` — 157 entries anitopy itself passes.
- `failing_table.py` — 35 entries anitopy is known to get wrong itself; the
  expected dict in each entry is still asserted-correct anitomy output
  (see anitopy's own `test_fails`), so these are valid ground truth for us
  even though anitopy's Python parser fails them.

## Building the fixture suites

`scripts/build_fixtures.py` converts each source above (old category names
-> current `ElementKind` names, see the script for the exact key mapping)
into its own independent fixture file:

- `anitomy/tests/fixtures/anitomy_develop.json`
- `anitomy/tests/fixtures/anitomy_master.json`
- `anitomy/tests/fixtures/anitopy.json`

Each suite is tested **separately** (see `anitomy/tests/conformance.rs` and
`anitomy-py/tests/test_conformance.py`) — there is no cross-suite dedup or
merging. The same input filename may legitimately appear in more than one
suite; that's expected, since each suite asserts conformance against a
different upstream ground truth.

A handful of entries (2 in `anitomy-master/`, 2
in `anitopy/`) exercise per-case options (`allowed_delimiters`,
`ignored_strings`) that no longer exist in the current `Options` API — those
stay in the generated fixture file with a `"skip"` field explaining why, so
they show up as explicit skips in test output instead of vanishing.

`anitomy/tests/fixtures/self_rolled.json` is the fourth suite: hand-written
cases that aren't sourced from any upstream project (e.g. regressions found
during porting). It is **not** generated by `build_fixtures.py` — edit it
directly.
